title: Phase Control at Long Inter-Pulse Spacing
description: Raw BCTDS homodyne I/Q measurements of a two-pulse phase coherent-control protocol on a sapphire sample with spin-coated Shipley 1813 photoresist, driven at 3.4 GHz. The phase of the first pulse is swept through a full turn while the inter-pulse delay is varied from 10 to 600 ns, spanning the ~100 ns ring-down lifetime, to show how coherent control is lost as the delay exceeds the bath's memory. Only the in-phase (I) and quadrature (Q) signals are measured; magnitude, phase, and FFT spectra are computable from them and are not stored.

samples.name: Shipley
samples.material: Sapphire substrate with spin-coated Shipley 1813 photoresist

apparatus.waveguide: WR-229 rectangular waveguide, 3-6 GHz passband, TE10 mode

pulse_geometry.frame: raw-trace nanoseconds, the same frame as timestamp_ns
pulse_geometry.note: The second pulse is fixed in time for every spacing; the first pulse moves earlier as the delay grows. The post-pulse ring-down therefore begins at the same absolute time in every file, so timestamp_ns is directly comparable across spacings.
pulse_geometry.source: Measured from the pulse envelope in the raw data (threshold at 30% of peak magnitude): samples 442 -> 461.

inter_pulse_spacing.note: The inter-pulse delay spans 10 to 600 ns, bracketing the ~100 ns ring-down lifetime.

pulse_phase.note: Read from pulse_phase_array in each .npz, not assumed. 0 and 360 deg are the same physical point, acquired independently.

constant_drive_parameters.amplitude_note: This experiment uses the same two-pulse protocol as Experiment 4, but does not restate the drive amplitudes, and they appear in neither the filenames nor the .npz contents. Experiment 4 used 30000 a.u. (first pulse) and 10000 a.u. (second); that is NOT asserted here. Confirm with the team before relying on it.

overlap_with_other_experiments.experiment_12: These 9 files are byte-identical to the 3400 MHz half of experiment 12's 8 mK folder (verified by hash). Experiment 5 is the 8 mK / 3400 MHz slice of the same acquisition campaign that produced Experiment 12 (shared run ID 'Shipley_phase_8mK_20251012_132853'). Both are released separately so each dataset stands alone.

acquisition.elapsed_s_note: elapsed_s is wall-clock seconds since run_start_utc, taken from the time_stamp_list array inside each .npz (one entry per phase point). Stored as elapsed seconds rather than an absolute epoch because float32 cannot represent Unix timestamps to better than ~128 s.
